route_candidates.name: London Stock Exchange ? Issuers and Instruments Reports
route_candidates.expected_coverage: Potentially useful for UK and international instruments admitted to LSE markets, depending on available report exports.
route_candidates.why: After OTC produced only 25 net-new rows, the next route should target an official venue/reporting source with broader coverage than a small screener result.
route_candidates.planned_treatment: v2.10B must inspect/report planned LSE acquisition routes without downloading. v2.10C may download only routes approved by v2.10B.

route_candidates.name: Cboe Europe Equities Reference Data
route_candidates.expected_coverage: Potentially useful for pan-European reference data if downloadable instruments files are exposed.
route_candidates.why: Official European exchange/operator source. Kept as fallback if LSE route is blocked or too dynamic.
route_candidates.planned_treatment: Keep as fallback for v2.11A or switch route only if v2.10B blocks LSE.

route_candidates.name: DataHub NYSE Other Listings
route_candidates.expected_coverage: Likely overlap with existing Nasdaq Trader otherlisted data and may be too small.
route_candidates.why: Useful as reference or QA comparison, but not ideal as next primary provider because it is not the primary exchange source and likely overlaps.
route_candidates.planned_treatment: Reference/QA only unless official routes fail.

route_candidates.name: Nasdaq Stock Screener Revisit
route_candidates.expected_coverage: Likely overlaps with existing Nasdaq Trader and SEC sources.
route_candidates.why: Could enrich names/metadata, but less promising for large net-new expansion.
route_candidates.planned_treatment: Keep deferred unless LSE and Cboe Europe fail.

planned_controls: No expanded_universe rebuild in v2.10A or v2.10B.
Prefer official downloadable CSV/XLS/XLSX/report files over dynamic page scraping.
If LSE route is dynamic or blocked, close as blocked and switch to Cboe Europe route.
validation_questions: Does LSE expose a stable downloadable issuer/instrument report?
What file format is available: CSV, XLS, XLSX, HTML table, ZIP, PDF?
Does the report contain a usable symbol, issuer name, instrument type, market/segment and country?
Does the report include ordinary shares, ETFs, funds, bonds or mixed instruments?
Can rows be normalized to the canonical source schema without brittle scraping?
How many rows are available before net-new filtering?
How many exchange+ticker or MIC+ticker keys are net-new against expanded_universe_v2_8e?
Does LSE unlock the 15000-row first expansion threshold?
Should LSE be treated as source provider, candidate provider, enrichment, reference-only or deferred?
warnings: Current expanded source remains below first expansion threshold: 9200 < 15000.
Current expanded source remains below full-source threshold: 9200 < 50000.
v2.10A is route-selection only; do not download LSE or Cboe Europe data in this phase.
LSE may contain multiple report formats, archives or dynamic pages; v2.10B must define a controlled acquisition contract before network access.
International venues may introduce non-US instruments, currencies, MICs and symbol semantics; classification must be conservative.
recommendation: Proceed to v2.10B LSE Acquisition Plan. Do not download or rebuild until the controlled acquisition contract is defined.
